## Benjamin Thompson

Senior Software Engineer — Front-End Architecture, Component Systems, UI/UX · San Antonio, TX  
ben@cxii.us · cxii.us · linkedin.com/in/cxiius · dribbble.com/cxiius

## Summary

Benjamin Thompson is a strategic, results-driven senior front-end engineer and UI/UX designer with over 20 years of experience building accessible, responsive, enterprise-grade web applications. He combines engineering discipline with design judgment to architect component systems where brand, accessibility, and interaction standards are enforced by structure rather than convention, ensuring intuitive, goal-oriented user experiences. Known as an innovative business partner and effective communicator, he translates complex requirements into seamless, high-performance solutions that integrate front-end and back-end systems.

He specializes in designing systems that support AI-orchestrated development workflows, creating scalable component libraries and guardrails that enable both developers and AI agents to produce consistent, high-quality code while preserving design fidelity and system integrity. His work focuses on making data-heavy, security-focused applications clear, usable, and accessible.

A collaborative leader, US Coast Guard Reserve Veteran, and resourceful problem solver, he has a proven track record of guiding cross-functional teams to deliver efficient code, leverage emerging technologies, and drive business-critical initiatives.

## Experience

**Lead UI Developer — LevelBlue** · Dallas, TX (remote / contract) · Jul 2025 – Apr 2026  
Embedded on a four-person strike team brought in to recover a cybersecurity product stalled for over a year, with a mandate to ship a fully functional application within six months.

- Owns end-to-end interface design for the rescued product.
- Architects an opinionated Angular 21 component library that makes inconsistent or off-brand UI structurally difficult to produce, codifying color, typography, spacing, and interaction patterns.
- Designs the library’s API and consumption patterns for human developers and AI agents alike, preserving accessibility and design fidelity regardless of who implements features.
- Supports a team environment where Cursor is the primary AI development environment and models are chosen per task type; the library’s structure anchors reliable AI-assisted delivery at scale, compressing feature timelines from months to weeks while meeting enterprise quality expectations.

**Outcome:** Delivers the previously stalled application within the six-month window and establishes an architecture that supports multi-model AI-assisted development without sacrificing consistency or accessibility.

**Senior Software Engineer — Fortra** (formerly Digital Defense, Inc.) · Eden Prairie, MN (remote) · May 2013 – Jan 2025 · Full-time  
One of two front-end developers re-engineering Frontline—DDI’s flagship SaaS vulnerability management platform—from the ground up on Angular 1.x; later works through DDI’s acquisition by Fortra on Frontline and related products.

- Acts as primary UI/UX designer for Frontline, making a data-intensive security product approachable for practitioners.
- Champions responsive design against internal pushback; adoption becomes a recurring strength in client demos.
- Builds and maintains internal Angular and React component libraries used across Frontline and other web applications; continues to own significant front-end architecture and library maintenance through the acquisition.
- Designs and delivers POCs and MVPs for new internal revenue streams independently.
- Post-acquisition: implements Figma designs in production, then serves on a strike team as primary front-end developer for new features and application development.

**Outcome:** Contributes to a product recognized by analysts (e.g., Gartner, Frost & Sullivan) and SC Magazine “Best Buy” for ease of use—cited as a factor in Fortra’s acquisition of DDI—across eleven-plus years of continuous delivery across organizational change.

**Principal UI/UX Designer — MOD Systems** · Seattle, WA · Feb 2006 – Apr 2010 · Full-time  
Sole designer for a media-on-demand platform (retail kiosks, mobile, set-top) bridging physical and digital sales before streaming dominated the market.

- Designs the full UX across kiosks, mobile, and set-top experiences, including kiosk deployments with partners such as Starbucks HEAR Music, Best Buy, Blockbuster, Hollywood Video, and Circuit City; contributes to concepts that help secure digital rights from major studios and networks.
- Directs contract design teams and produces investor-facing and marketing materials.

**Outcome:** Serves as the single creative lead for a nationally deployed platform; the company winds down as the industry moves away from physical media.

**Web Designer and Developer — Independent Contractor** · San Antonio, TX · Nov 2010 – Apr 2013 · Freelance  
Delivers full-cycle web design and development for small businesses and nonprofits: discovery through deployment and maintenance, custom WordPress themes and bespoke CMS work, branding, and clear communication with non-technical clients.

**Outcome:** Ships end-to-end sites and CMS solutions for clients including the University of Colorado Bookstore, Extelligence Internet Services, and other independent organizations.

## Skills

**Languages & markup:** HTML, CSS, SASS/SCSS, JavaScript, TypeScript; PHP (proficient; primary use in freelance period).

**Frameworks & libraries:** Angular (through Angular 21), React, SvelteKit, Web Components; Bootstrap, Material UI, TailwindCSS; RxJS; jQuery (deep historical use); WordPress.

**Component systems & design:** Component library architecture, design systems, UI/UX design, Figma (design-to-code and prototyping), Storybook, responsive design, accessibility (WCAG).

**Testing & quality:** Jest, Vitest, Karma, Testing Library, MSW, unit and component testing.

**Build tools & toolchain:** Vite, Webpack, Nx (monorepo in enterprise Angular contexts).

**Backend & APIs:** Node.js, Next.js, Express.js, REST, GraphQL.

**Databases:** MySQL.

**DevOps & cloud:** Docker, Cloudflare, CI/CD pipelines.

**AI-assisted development:** Cursor, multi-model workflows, AI-compatible component API design.

**Collaboration & process:** Git, GitHub, GitLab, Figma collaboration, Atlassian (Jira, Confluence), Notion, Agile/Scrum, Kanban.

## Education

**Associate of Science, Digital Media — Full Sail University** · Winter Park, FL · 2005  
Class Valedictorian; Advanced Achiever Award; Certificate of Excellence; Perfect Attendance Award. Featured in the inaugural issue of the school’s quarterly publication.

**Web Design Certificate — Sessions.edu** · Online · 2000  
Project artwork featured in school promotional materials for several years after completion.

## Projects

**What’s For Dinner** (active development; private beta planned)  
Offline-capable Svelte 5 / SvelteKit app that suggests meals from the user’s own recipe library, balancing time of day, preparation frequency, and variety so favorites and long-ignored recipes both surface. Dexie for local storage; Supabase for auth and sync. Includes an in-app assistant for new recipes and in-place edits with dietary and method preferences. Roadmap: private beta, then public web and an Ionic companion app.

## Background

**US Coast Guard Reserve** (2008–2016) · Boatswain’s Mate, Petty Officer Third Class · Everett, WA; Port O’Connor, TX  
Served as small-boat crew and boarding team member; completed ‘A’ School (Yorktown, VA, 2009); Navigation Rules Ace Award; Search & Rescue Fundamentals training.

**Martial arts:** 1st Degree Black Belt, Tracy’s Kenpo (Professional Self Defense of Durango, CO, 2003).
